title: Bridge Essay: Feedback signals in visual cortex during episodic and schematic memory retrieval

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Feedback, signals, in, visual, cortex.

**Feedback signals in visual cortex during episodic and schematic memory retrieval and their potential implications for aphantasia.** — Neurosci Biobehav Rev. PMID: 37524137. DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2023.105335. Recent findings indicate that visual feedback derived from episodic memory can be traced down to the earliest stages of visual processing, whereas feedback stemming from schema-related memories only reach intermediate levels in the visual processing hierarchy. In this opinion piece, we examine these differences in light of the 'what' and 'where' streams of visual perception. We build upon this new framework to propose that the memory deficits observed in aphantasics might be better understood as
